description: This figure presents atom probe tomography (APT) characterization results of 1.3Al low alloy ultrahigh strength steel, which was processed by 970 °C solution treatment for 1 h followed by water quenching, then tempering at 180 °C for 2 h and water quenching. The central 3D reconstructed atom map with 20 nm scale bar marks 10 at.% NiAl isoconcentration surfaces (green) and 5 at.% C isoconcentration surfaces (brown). Subfigure A shows Ni, Al, C atomic distribution across an ε-carbide cross-section. Subfigure B is a proximity histogram for dispersed NiAl clusters, showing Ni and Al concentrations rise inside clusters while C concentration remains ~1 at.% around clusters. Subfigure C displays 4 at.% Cr+Mo, 7 at.% Si isoconcentration surfaces and a proximity histogram for segregated NiAl clusters, showing Si, Cr, Mo enrich around NiAl clusters. The results confirm ε-carbides and NiAl clusters nucleate and grow independently during tempering without competitive partitioning between C and Ni/Al, and Si-enriched regions provide favorable nucleation sites for NiAl clusters.
